creator: dirk.meyer@dinoex.sub.org
text:
Florent Thoumie schrieb:,

> >  All GNUstep ports that generates shared libs.
> >  they use:
> >  LDCONFIG_DIRS+= ${i}
> >  INSTALLS_SHLIB= yes
> >
> >  I think it would be cleaner to use:
> >  USE_LDCONFIG+= ${i}
> >  and preserve the old functionality,
> >  so INSTALLS_SHLIB= can be deprecated.
> 
> What old functionality? I still don't see any LOCALBASE reference in
> bsd.port.mk.

INSTALLS_SHLIB does record the path in pkg-plist.
USE_LDCONFIG des record the path in a textfile.
This textfiles resides in LOCALBASE.

> That being said, I see:
> 
> GNUSTEP_PREFIX?=        ${LOCALBASE}/GNUstep
> 
> ... which is wrong.

I don't agree on this.

> And as I was saying in that related PR, I think it's really bad
> practice for ports to override PREFIX.

PREFIX ist still honored.

> >  This will be be useful for the linux ports as well.
> >
> >  > 2. If you set NO_MTREE in your port, it's your responsibility to make
> >  > sure these directories exist and are deleted at deinstall time.
> >
> >  I would create them, but they will not be parsed,
> >  so its is just dead weight.
> 
> I don't understand what you mean here.

If I create this extra directory:
a) this will confuse the GNUstep hier.
b) The files inside this will not be parsed.
c) The user setup will get much more complicated,
   so the instructions on www.gnustep.org will no longer apply.

> >  > 3. This is normal behaviour, if you're using alternative prefixes, set
> >  > ldconfig_local{,32}_dirs in /etc/rc.conf.
> >
> >  This would break POLA for all GNUstep users.
> >  Which I don't want to do without good reason.
> 
> This is different, ldconfig_local_dirs is set to a list of prefixes
> (/usr/local, /usr/X11R6, /opt ...).
> If gnustep installs shared libraries in /usr/local/lib/gnustep, it's
> still /usr/local prefix, and a file in /usr/local/libdata/ldconfig can
> be created without having to override ldconfig_local_dirs.

/usr/local/lib/gnustep is out of options,
unless we rewite a good part of GNUstep infrastucture.

If this bothers portsmgr, I will remove the offending ports from CVS.
